steps.title: Track the contrast
steps.content: The underlined portion begins with But, so it counters the earlier criticism of abrupt endings. Instead of treating the endings as arbitrary flaws, it says the Aubrey/Maturin books can be viewed as one long work.

steps.title: Separate scope from distortion
steps.content: A adds unsupported claims about most critics and literary renown. C adds entertainment and treats the structure as a flaw, which the underlined portion does not do. D narrows the point to HMS Surprise and invents a claim about being entertaining. The remaining choice describes a favorable view of the series' structure. The answer is B.
